name: Zasu Pitts' Chocolate Dipped Fondant
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Dipping/coating chocolate (shaved into small pieces)
recipeInstructions:
BASIC FONDANT
1. Heat water to boiling in a 2- to 3-quart saucepan.
2. Remove from heat and add sugar and cream of tartar.
3. Stir with wooden spoon until all sugar dissolves, about 6 to 8 minutes.
4. Place syrup mixture over medium-high heat.
5. Heat almost to boiling, covering pan tightly with lid.
6. Remove from heat; let stand 3 minutes.
7. Uncover, return to heat and boil briskly without moving the pan.
8. Wash any crystals on sides of pan using a damp cloth wrapped around a fork.
9. Continue cooking until syrup reaches soft-ball stage (238°F) in candy thermometer, about 35 minutes.
10. Remove from heat; let stand a few minutes until bubbles disappear.
11. Pour onto marble slab or large shallow platter and knead with hands until smooth white ball of fondant forms.
12. Wrap fondant in plastic and store in glass jar covered tightly. Let mellow 24 hours to 3 days.
CHOCOLATE-DIPPED FONDANT
1. Place chocolate in top part of double boiler.
2. In lower part, place enough warm water (100-120°F) to touch the pan.
3. Keep water temperature constant; melting takes time.
4. Stir vigorously until smallest lumps are worked out.
5. Exchange warm water for cooler water (85°F) in bottom of double boiler.
6. Ensure chocolate does not come in contact with steam or water.
7. Pour 1 cup chocolate onto marble slab or small bowl and work back and forth with fingers until it feels cool (83°F is perfect).
8. Test by letting a small amount string from your hand; if it holds its shape briefly, it's ready.
